facts.text: A railroad station built in 1861 was named Kennedy, and the name was restored in 1860 at an Erie Railroad official’s request.
The 1896 Massapequa station, built by the Floyd‑Jones family, later served as a sales center for developers marketing lots to buyers.
In the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries, Massapequa grew into a small resort community that welcomed visitors from Manhattan and Brooklyn.
A 1969 drainage project uncovered 184 jasper blades dated between 75 BCE and 400 CE, the only confirmed evidence of Woodland‑period activity in the area.
Massapequa’s temperate climate mirrors other coastal Northeastern U.S. areas, with warm humid summers, cold winters, and Atlantic sea breezes that temper heat.
